behavioral_patterns.value:
API compromise leading to download link manipulation

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Compromise secondary API/feature of legitimate software vendor
  • Step 2: Modify download links on official website
  • Step 3: Redirect users to malicious file hosting

Detection: Monitor for unexpected changes to download URLs on legitimate software sites, especially redirects to external storage services
behavioral_patterns.context: API compromise leading to download link manipulation

behavioral_patterns.value:
Supply chain attack via compromised distribution infrastructure

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Gain access to software distribution system
  • Step 2: Replace legitimate download links with malicious ones
  • Step 3: Serve trojanized versions while keeping original files intact

Detection: Compare file hashes of downloads against known good versions and monitor for unexpected hosting locations
behavioral_patterns.context: Supply chain attack via compromised distribution infrastructure

behavioral_patterns.value:
File masquerading with legitimate software branding

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Create malicious executable with legitimate software name
  • Step 2: Host on external storage service
  • Step 3: Serve via compromised official download portal

Detection: Validate digital signatures and detect files with legitimate names hosted on unexpected domains
behavioral_patterns.context: File masquerading with legitimate software branding

behavioral_patterns.value:
Multi-staged malware deployment with memory execution

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Deliver initial trojanized installer
  • Step 2: Execute multi-stage loading process
  • Step 3: Operate primarily in-memory to avoid disk artifacts

Detection: Monitor for suspicious process injection and memory-only execution patterns from installers
behavioral_patterns.context: Multi-staged malware deployment with memory execution

behavioral_patterns.value:
EDR/AV evasion through NTDLL proxying

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Load malicious payload in memory
  • Step 2: Proxy NTDLL functionality through .NET assembly
  • Step 3: Evade detection by masking API calls

Detection: Monitor for unusual .NET assembly loading patterns and indirect NTDLL access

Opportunistic timing exploitation during maintenance windows

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Reconnaissance of target organization schedules
  • Step 2: Time attack during developer absence/holidays
  • Step 3: Exploit reduced monitoring and response capabilities

Detection: Implement continuous monitoring that doesn't rely on human oversight during off-hours
behavioral_patterns.context: Opportunistic timing exploitation during maintenance windows

behavioral_patterns.value:
Cross-platform utility targeting campaign

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Identify widely-used utility software
  • Step 2: Compromise distribution channels
  • Step 3: Repeat pattern across multiple popular tools

Detection: Monitor for similar compromise patterns across different popular utility software vendors

Cloudflare R2 abuse for malware hosting

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Upload malicious files to Cloudflare R2 storage
  • Step 2: Configure compromised site to redirect to R2 URLs
  • Step 3: Leverage legitimate CDN reputation for distribution

Detection: Monitor for unexpected redirects from software sites to cloud storage services
behavioral_patterns.context: Cloudflare R2 abuse for malware hosting

behavioral_patterns.value:
Russian installer deployment with setup wrapper

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Package malware with Russian language installer
  • Step 2: Wrap with Inno Setup to appear legitimate
  • Step 3: Execute installation routine to deploy payload

Detection: Flag installers with unexpected language settings or geographic inconsistencies
behavioral_patterns.context: Russian installer deployment with setup wrapper

behavioral_patterns.value:
Selective link poisoning while preserving originals

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Compromise download distribution system
  • Step 2: Poison primary download links
  • Step 3: Leave direct URLs intact to avoid immediate detection

Detection: Compare files served through different download paths and monitor for inconsistencies
behavioral_patterns.context: Selective link poisoning while preserving originals

behavioral_patterns.value:
Information stealing through trojanized system utilities

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Compromise popular system monitoring tools
  • Step 2: Deploy infostealer payload alongside legitimate functionality
  • Step 3: Harvest system information during normal tool usage

Detection: Monitor system utilities for unexpected network connections or data exfiltration
behavioral_patterns.context: Information stealing through trojanized system utilities

behavioral_patterns.value:
Advanced loader deployment with known TTPs

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Deploy sophisticated loader malware
  • Step 2: Implement established attack techniques
  • Step 3: Execute payload delivery using proven methods

Detection: Correlate loader behaviors with known TTP databases and threat intelligence feeds
behavioral_patterns.context: Advanced loader deployment with known TTPs

-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 6 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Behavior: especially redirects to external storage services
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-aaf9cd62
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'especially redirects to external storage services' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: especially redirects to external storage services
-- Source: behavioral_0
-- TTP: T1036.005
-- Kill Chain: 
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'especially redirects to external storage services' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1036.005' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_0' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bespecially\ redirects\ to\ external\ storage\ services\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bespecially\ redirects\ to\ external\ storage\ services\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 12 rules from 12 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - API compromise leading to download link manipulation
// Category: defense_evasion
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1036.005
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Set-MpPreference", "DisableRealtimeMonitoring", "-ep bypass")
    or FileName in~ ("reg.exe", "attrib.exe")
| summarize EventCount = count(),
    UniqueCommands = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, AccountName
| sort by EventCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - Supply chain attack via compromised distribution infrastruct
// Category: supply_chain
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1195
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("npm.exe", "pip.exe", "pip3.exe", "nuget.exe")
    or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("install", "update", "upgrade")
| summarize InstallCount = count(),
    UniquePackages = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where InstallCount >= 5
| sort by InstallCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - File masquerading with legitimate software branding
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Multi-staged malware deployment with memory execution
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - EDR/AV evasion through NTDLL proxying
// Category: defense_evasion
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1562
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Set-MpPreference", "DisableRealtimeMonitoring", "-ep bypass")
    or FileName in~ ("reg.exe", "attrib.exe")
| summarize EventCount = count(),
    UniqueCommands = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, AccountName
| sort by EventCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - Opportunistic timing exploitation during maintenance windows
// Category: discovery
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1595
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where FileName in~ ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| summarize ReconCount = count(),
    UniqueTools = dcount(FileName),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where ReconCount >= 3 or UniqueTools >= 2
| sort by ReconCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Cross-platform utility targeting campaign
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 8: Behavioral Detection - Cloudflare R2 abuse for malware hosting
// Category: defense_evasion
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1036.005
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Set-MpPreference", "DisableRealtimeMonitoring", "-ep bypass")
    or FileName in~ ("reg.exe", "attrib.exe")
| summarize EventCount = count(),
    UniqueCommands = dcount(ProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, AccountName
| sort by EventCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 9: Behavioral Detection - Russian installer deployment with setup wrapper
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 10: Behavioral Detection - Selective link poisoning while preserving originals
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 11: Behavioral Detection - Information stealing through trojanized system utilities
// Category: exfiltration
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1041
CloudAppEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ActionType in ("FileUploaded", "FileSyncUploadedFull", "FileDownloaded")
| summarize UploadCount = count(),
    TotalBytes = sum(RawEventData.FileSize),
    UniqueFiles = dcount(ObjectName),
    FirstEvent = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastEvent = max(TimeGenerated)
    by AccountDisplayName, Application
| where UploadCount >= 10 or UniqueFiles >= 5
| sort by UploadCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 12: Behavioral Detection - Advanced loader deployment with known TTPs
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 12 rules from 12 indicators

``` Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - API compromise leading to download link manipulation
``` Category: defense_evasion
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1036.005
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process (process_command_line="*Set-MpPreference*" OR process_command_line="*DisableRealtimeMonitoring*" OR process_command_line="*-ep bypass*")
| stats count as event_count, dc(process_command_line) as unique_cmds, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| sort -event_count
| table dest, user, event_count, unique_cmds, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - Supply chain attack via compromised distribution infrastruct
``` Category: supply_chain
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1195
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process process_name IN ("npm.exe", "pip.exe", "pip3.exe", "nuget.exe") (process_command_line="*install*" OR process_command_line="*update*")
| stats count as install_count, dc(process_command_line) as unique_packages, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| where install_count >= 5
| sort -install_count
| table dest, user, install_count, unique_packages, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - File masquerading with legitimate software branding
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Multi-staged malware deployment with memory execution
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user

| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

compiled_outputs.sigma:
# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)
# Generated: 12 rules from 12 indicators
# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - API compromise leading to download link manipulation'
id: 19d4898e-d6b8-54c5-8a72-091f787a5d6a
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
    - attack.t1036.005
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        CommandLine|contains|any:
            - 'Set-MpPreference'
            - 'DisableRealtimeMonitoring'
            - '-ep bypass'
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity

---

title: 'KTLYST - Supply chain attack via compromised distribution infrastruct'
id: 46a1bc39-edd9-561f-9a0c-8550f883c830
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
    - attack.t1195
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: any
detection:
    selection_process:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\npm.exe'
            - '\pip.exe'
            - '\nuget.exe'
    selection_action:
        CommandLine|contains|any:
            - 'install'
            - 'update'
    condition: selection_process and selection_action
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity
